title: How to Apply for Driving License Online — Learner's to Permanent DL Complete Guide
meta_description: Driving license 2026 — apply online via Parivahan portal, Learner's License, permanent DL, renewal, documents, driving test, RTO slot booking.

hero.one_liner: Get driving license online through Parivahan portal — Learner's License first, then permanent DL after practice and driving test at RTO

sections.heading: Driving License Overview — Types & Timeline
content:
LEARNER'S LICENSE (LL):
- For: Learning to drive before applying for permanent license
- Validity: 6 months from date of issue
- Fee: ₹200
- Requirements: 18+ years for 4-wheeler (LMV), 16+ years for 2-wheeler (MCWG)
- Process: Fill Form 2 online → Upload documents → Pay fee → RTO slot booking → LL test (MCQs on traffic rules) → LL issued same day
- LL test: 15-20 multiple-choice questions on road signs, traffic rules, vehicle basics.

Most people pass easily.
- Validity: Must apply for permanent DL within 6 months of LL issue. After 6 months, LL expires and you need a fresh LL if you didn't apply for DL.

PERMANENT DRIVING LICENSE (DL):
- For: Actual driving on public roads
- Validity: 20 years OR until age 50 (whichever comes first) for non-commercial. For commercial, different validity.

- Fee: ₹400-600 (varies by state and vehicle class)
- Requirement: Must have valid LL for at least 30 days before applying for DL
- Process: Fill Form 4 online → Book driving test → Visit RTO for practical test → DL issued after passing
- Driving test: Practical test of actual driving skills.
Includes: forward driving, reversing, parking, turning, and sometimes road driving. Differs by RTO — each has specific track layout.

DL RENEWAL:
- For: Existing DL expiring or expired within 3 years
- Validity: Another 20 years from renewal date
- Fee: ₹200-300
- Requirement: Medical certificate (Form 1A) after age 40
- Process: Simple online renewal — no test needed (assuming you haven't been disqualified)

steps.title: Visit parivahan.gov.in (Sarathi/Vahan portal)
steps.description: Go to official Parivahan (Ministry of Road Transport) portal. Select your state from dropdown. Click 'Online Services' → 'Apply for Learner License'.

steps.title: Register or login
steps.description: First-time users: Create account with email and mobile. Verify email and OTP. Existing users: Just login.

steps.title: Fill Learner's License application (Form 2) online
steps.description: Enter: Full name, date of birth, father's name, address, mobile number, email, blood group, educational qualification. Choose vehicle class: LMV (light motor vehicle — car/jeep), MCWG (motorcycle with gear), MWCOG (2-wheeler without gear), or BOTH (if wanting to learn both).

steps.title: Upload age proof document
steps.description: Aadhaar card (best), OR Birth certificate, OR Passport, OR 10th marksheet showing DOB. Must be 18+ for car, 16+ for 2-wheeler. Scan and upload as PDF/JPEG (max 1MB).

steps.title: Upload address proof
steps.description: Aadhaar card (best), OR Utility bill (within 3 months), OR Bank statement, OR Passport. Address must match application.

steps.title: Upload medical certificate (Form 1A) — if required by state
steps.description: For commercial vehicles (heavy truck, bus, auto-rickshaw), medical certificate is required. For personal car/bike, usually optional (but having one doesn't hurt). Get it from any licensed medical practitioner — simple form, costs ₹200-500.

steps.title: Pay fee and submit
steps.description: Fee: ₹200. Pay via net banking, debit card, UPI, or SBI challan. System generates application number and confirmation.

steps.title: Book RTO appointment for LL test
steps.description: After payment, book slot at your nearest RTO/SDM office. Calendar shows available dates. Choose convenient date. Most RTOs have slots daily.

steps.title: Visit RTO on appointment date for LL test
steps.description: Carry: Printed application receipt, original ID proof (Aadhaar), address proof. At RTO: Give test — 15-20 MCQs on traffic signs, road rules, vehicle safety. Test is on computer at RTO. Questions like: meaning of yellow line on road, speed limit on highway, right-of-way rules, etc. Need 60% to pass.

steps.title: LL issued same-day on passing
steps.description: If you pass (need 60%), LL is issued immediately. You can drive with LL on public roads only if accompanied by a licensed driver (sitting beside you). After 30 days of LL issue, you can apply for permanent DL.

sections.heading: Step 2: Apply for Permanent DL — Form 4

steps.title: Wait minimum 30 days after LL issue
steps.description: You MUST wait at least 30 days from LL issue date before applying for DL. If you apply on day 25, application will be rejected. Mark the calendar: LL issued on Jan 1 → Can apply DL from Jan 31 onwards.

steps.title: Practice driving at your RTO's test track
steps.description: Visit your RTO office and ask for the test track location. Most RTOs have specific driving tracks with marked lanes. Practice: forward driving, reverse parking, figure-8 driving (for 2-wheelers), three-point turn, etc. Each RTO's track layout is different — practice on the EXACT track where your test will be held.

steps.title: Visit parivahan.gov.in → Apply for Driving License (Form 4)
steps.description: Login → Select 'Apply for Permanent Driving License' → Enter your LL number → System retrieves your details. Fill any updated information. No need to re-enter everything — mostly pre-filled.

steps.title: Upload documents for DL
steps.description: Similar to LL: age proof, address proof. LL itself serves as age proof, so minimal new docs needed. Any updated docs only.

steps.title: Pay DL fee online
steps.description: LMV (car): ₹400. MCWG (2-wheeler with gear): ₹400. MWCOG (2-wheeler without gear): ₹300. Both vehicle classes: ₹600-700. Fee includes smart card DL cost. Pay online.

steps.title: Book driving test appointment at RTO
steps.description: After payment, book appointment for your driving test. Choose date when you feel ready. Most RTOs have test slots daily. Some busy RTOs may have slots weeks ahead.

steps.title: Visit RTO on test day with vehicle and documents
steps.description: Bring: LL (original), vehicle (your car/bike), documents, appointment confirmation. Arrive 10-15 minutes early. Testing usually starts at 9-10 AM.

steps.title: Driving test — practical driving skills assessment
steps.description:
Test format varies by RTO. Generally includes: (1) Forward driving on marked lane (straightforward), (2) Reversing (backing up in straight line), (3) Parking (parallel parking or in-lot parking), (4) Turning and handling curves, (5) For 2-wheelers: Figure-8 test (tight circle maneuvering). Test takes 10-20 minutes. Test track is usually small (200m x 50m) with marked paths — not open-road driving.

Testing Points: Vehicle control, smoothness of acceleration/braking, horn usage, mirror checking. Automatic failures: Hitting cones/marks, stalling engine, hitting boundaries, rash acceleration, wrong horn usage. Most people need 2-3 attempts before passing.

steps.title: If passing, DL is issued within 7-30 days
steps.description: After passing test, you get a receipt. Smart card DL (plastic card) is printed and mailed to your address within 7-30 days via post. E-DL (digital copy) is available on Parivahan/DigiLocker immediately — you can use e-DL right away. DL validity: 20 years OR until age 50 (whichever earlier).

steps.title: If failing, retake test after 7 days
steps.description: You can retake the test after 7 days. There's no limit on retakes as long as your LL is valid (6-month validity). Each retry costs ₹200. Many people fail first attempt due to nervousness — second/third attempt usually passes.

sections.heading: DL Driving Test Tips — How to Pass

steps.title: Visit RTO test track before your test date
steps.description: Go 2-3 days before your test appointment. Visit the actual RTO test track (without vehicle, just observe). Watch the marked lanes, understand the path, see other drivers practicing. This removes 50% of nervousness.

steps.title: Practice figure-8 and reverse parking multiple times
steps.description: Most people fail on: (1) Reverse parking — practice backing straight and smooth, (2) Figure-8 for 2-wheelers — maintain slow speed and smooth steering. Practice these 20-30 times before test. Other maneuvers are easier.

steps.title: Drive at slow, smooth speed during test
steps.description: Test isn't about speed — it's about control. Drive at 10-20 km/h. Avoid jerky acceleration/braking. Smooth is safe.

steps.title: Check mirrors frequently during test
steps.description: Show the examiner you're aware of surroundings. Check side mirrors, rear-view mirror. Don't forget this — it's a passing criterion.

steps.title: Use horn at corners and before moving
steps.description: Indian road culture emphasizes horn usage. Use horn before starting, at turns, before reversing. Don't honk excessively — 2-3 times is enough at each point.

steps.title: Avoid hitting cones or boundary marks
steps.description: Cones/marks define your driving lane. If you hit even one cone, you may fail. Drive carefully within the marked area. Bumping a cone is automatic failure in some RTOs.

steps.title: If you stall the engine, restart and continue
steps.description: Stalling once is not automatic failure in most RTOs. Restart the vehicle and continue. Multiple stalls might fail you. Keep calm.

steps.title: Second/third attempt success rate is very high
steps.description: Most people pass on 2nd or 3rd attempt. First attempt: nervous, mistakes. Second: more confident, fewer mistakes, usually passes. Don't be discouraged if you fail first time.

sections.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid
content: MISTAKE 1 — Going through agents/touts: Many touts outside RTO offer 'help' for ₹3,000-8,000. Don't.
The entire system is online now. You don't need agents.
Costs are transparent: LL ₹200, DL ₹400 — nothing more. MISTAKE 2 — Not practicing at exact RTO test track: Each RTO has different track layout.
If you practice at a different location's track, you'll be confused during actual test. Practice at YOUR RTO's track 5-10 times before test.
MISTAKE 3 — Applying for DL before 30 days of LL: Your application will be rejected. LL MUST be at least 30 days old.
If rejected, you need to reapply (fee ₹200 wasted). MISTAKE 4 — Letting LL expire: LL valid for 6 months only.
If you don't apply for DL within 6 months, LL expires. You need a fresh LL (pay ₹200 again) to apply for DL.
MISTAKE 5 — Not carrying LL on test day: LL is proof you're licensed to learn. Without it, test won't happen.
Keep LL in vehicle. MISTAKE 6 — Using automatic transmission car for test: Some states prefer manual transmission test (shows better control).
Check with RTO if manual is required. Using auto when manual expected may cause issues.
MISTAKE 7 — Not uploading medical certificate when required: For some vehicle classes or states, medical certificate (Form 1A) is mandatory. Without it, LL/DL won't be issued.

steps.title: Start renewal 1 year before expiry
steps.description: Don't wait until expiry. Start process 12 months before expiry date. If expired, renewal can take longer and you're not legally allowed to drive with expired DL.

steps.title: Get medical certificate (Form 1A) if age 40+
steps.description: After 40 years, medical certificate becomes mandatory. Visit licensed medical practitioner, get Form 1A signed. Cost: ₹200-500. Certificate is valid for 1 year.

steps.title: Visit parivahan.gov.in → DL Renewal
steps.description: Login → Select 'DL Renewal' → Enter DL number → Fill any updated information. No new documents usually needed (address verified from old DL).

steps.title: Pay renewal fee and book RTO appointment
steps.description: Fee: ₹200-300 (varies by state). Book appointment at your RTO for biometric update (fingerprint, eye scan if needed).

steps.title: Visit RTO for final processing
steps.description: Bring original DL + appointment confirmation. RTO takes fingerprints (biometric update) and confirms renewal. Processing: 5-15 days. Renewed DL mailed within 15-30 days.

steps.title: Apply at your RTO with Indian DL and passport
steps.description: Visit RTO → Request 'International Driving Permit'. Provide: Valid Indian DL, Passport, visa of destination country.

steps.title: Upload documents and pay fee
steps.description: Fee: ₹1,000. Pay online. IDP is valid for 1 year from date of issue. Some countries accept IDP; others require local license — check destination country's rule.

steps.title: IDP issued within 10-15 days
steps.description: Physical IDP booklet mailed to your address. Carry both IDP + Indian DL when driving abroad. IDP is just a translation/international version — still need to carry original Indian DL.

sections.heading: DL Validity & Duration Summary
rows: Example: DL issued at 25, valid until 45 (20 years) or 50 (age limit)
Must be renewed every 5 years. Medical cert required.
Age 60+: DL valid 1-3 years, then renew more frequently
Automatic conversion to adult DL at 18 (different validity)

sections.heading: Documents Checklist — LL & DL
rows: Aadhaar best, if address changed then utility bill
Not needed for personal car/bike usually
Photo taken at RTO overrides any submitted photo

sections.heading: Automatic Driving License Failures — What Disqualifies You

faqs.question: Do I need a lawyer or agent to get driving license?
faqs.answer: NO. Entire process is online and free of agents. LL costs ₹200, DL costs ₹400 — no extra charges. Agents charge ₹3,000-8,000 for something you can do yourself in 30 minutes. Do it yourself online.

faqs.question: Can I get driving license without LL?
faqs.answer: Not in most states. LL is mandatory — it's proof you know basic traffic rules. Some states allow direct DL (without LL) for people over 40 — check your state's specific rule. Generally: LL first (30 days waiting) → Then DL.

faqs.question: I failed LL test (didn't score 60%). What now?
faqs.answer: Retake after 7 days. There's no limit on LL test attempts as long as you're within the 6-month application validity. Each retry: same ₹200 fee. Questions are similar — study traffic rules more and retake.

faqs.question: I haven't driven in years and LL expired. Do I reapply?
faqs.answer: Yes. LL valid for 6 months. If expired, apply for fresh LL again (₹200). Then wait 30 days, apply for DL. The process repeats. It's better to apply for DL within 6 months of LL to avoid re-doing LL.

faqs.question: My DL is suspended/revoked due to driving under influence (DUI). Can I get it back?
faqs.answer: Suspension: Temporary — usually 6-12 months. After suspension period, DL is automatically restored if no new violations. Revocation: Permanent cancellation. To get new license, apply afresh (LL + DL, like a new person). Check court order to see if it's suspension or revocation.

faqs.question: Can I drive in other states with my DL?
faqs.answer: Yes. Indian DL is valid in ALL states. You can drive anywhere in India. However, if you permanently move to a new state and want to change your address on DL, apply for address change at new state's RTO — LL is invalidated when you move states.

faqs.question: I lost my DL. How to get duplicate?
faqs.answer: Apply at your RTO: 'Duplicate DL' → Pay ₹200 fee → Upload ID proof → Process takes 10-15 days. Alternatively, download e-DL from DigiLocker (free, instant) if state has digitized DLs — e-DL is legally valid everywhere.

disclaimer: Driving license procedures, fees, and validity as per Ministry of Road Transport guidelines as of March 2026. RTO practices vary by state — check your state RTO's website for exact procedures and requirements.
